entries.title: Heartbeat
entries.message: Loop ran. Day 2 forecast worsened (74 km/h gusts, 8.5mm rain), Day 3 unchanged dry & calm but freezing overnight. SafeTravel/Reynisfjara stable. Kp quiet. Next run in ~2h.

entries.title: Day 3 (Apr 10) overnight: first freeze of trip
entries.message: New: Day 3 low -0.3°C in Reykjavik. Combined with Day 2's 8.5mm rain, expect black ice on shaded pavement and bridge decks Apr 10 morning. If the rental car has summer tyres (most April rentals do), drive 5-10 km/h slower than feels right for the first hour out of Vík.

entries.title: Day 2 (Apr 9 Golden Circle) forecast worsened
entries.message: Open-Meteo new run: Day 2 gusts 74 km/h (was 66), rain 8.5mm (was 5.3). Day 1 slightly improved (67 km/h, 6mm). Day 3 unchanged — still nearly dry, 49 km/h. Translation: the wet/windy day shifted from arrival to Golden Circle. Þingvellir & Geysir steam plume photos will be challenging in 74 km/h gusts; Gullfoss spray will be brutal. Bring full waterproofs both legs and pants.

entries.title: Heartbeat
entries.message: Loop ran. Open-Meteo RECOVERED after 4 failed runs. 3-day outlook rosier than expected — Day 3 nearly dry. SafeTravel/Reynisfjara stable. YELLOW alert still active. Kp quiet. Next run in ~2h.

entries.title: Re-order Day 3: do walk-behind Seljalandsfoss early
entries.message: With Day 3 nearly dry (Open-Meteo 0.1mm) and wind down to 49 km/h gusts, the walk-behind path at Seljalandsfoss becomes safe and the rocks less slippery. Hit it at 09:00-10:00 before the Reykjavik tour buses (they arrive ~11:00). Skógafoss next, then Reynisfjara viewpoint in the afternoon. Vík overnight.

entries.title: Good news: Day 3 (Apr 10) much calmer & DRY
entries.message: Open-Meteo back online. 3-day outlook: Day 1 gusts 74 km/h / 8mm rain, Day 2 Golden Circle 66 km/h / 5mm rain, Day 3 South Coast 49 km/h / 0.1mm rain. Day 3 is the standout — nearly dry and wind drops ~34%. Reynisfjara will still be orange (sea takes days to calm), but Seljalandsfoss/Skógafoss walking will be much more pleasant. Save the long waterfall shoot for Day 3.

entries.title: Heartbeat
entries.message: Loop ran. vedur.is YELLOW alert confirms windy Day 1. Open-Meteo still 504 (4 runs), met.no fallback holding steady (3-5°C, 13 m/s, 15mm). Reynisfjara orange. Kp max 3.33. Next run in ~2h.

entries.title: Buy a cheap re-useable water bottle at KEF
entries.message: Iceland tap water is some of the cleanest on earth — direct from glacial springs, no need to buy bottled. Grab a 1L reusable (Krónan, Bónus, or airport Joe & the Juice ~ISK 500-1000) on Day 1 and refill everywhere. Saves ~ISK 400/bottle over 10 days and is better-tasting than anything bottled.

entries.title: Flood note: Héraðsvötn ice jam
entries.message: Per vedur.is: thawing + frozen ground causing rapid stream rise; ice jam risk in Héraðsvötn river (Skagafjörður, N Iceland) may break loose and flood the main road. NOT on our planned route (we stay south/west) but worth knowing if anyone suggests a detour north.

entries.title: vedur.is YELLOW alert: widespread wind/rain
entries.message: Per vedur.is/alerts (Apr 7): YELLOW weather alert covering Reykjavik Capital, South, Faxaflói, Breiðafjörður, Westfjords, NW, NE, East & Eastfjords — basically the whole country except the southeast. South 13-20 m/s + rain, moderating evening. Aligns with our forecast for Day 1 Reykjavik arrival. Plan indoor activities on landing.

entries.title: Heartbeat
entries.message: Loop ran. Deep sweep done: no Reykjanes eruption (magma building), QR corridor-restricted but on schedule, Day 1 wet/windy unchanged, Day 3 Vík still 16 m/s. Open-Meteo still 502 (3 runs), met.no working. Kp peaks 3.33 next 72h. Next run in ~2h.

entries.title: Photography: long-exposure waterfalls in low light
entries.message: Day 1-3 forecast = overcast, low light, 4-7°C. Perfect natural ND filter for silky-water shots at Seljalandsfoss/Skógafoss (Day 3). Settings starting point: ISO 100, f/11, 1-2 sec, tripod essential. Spray-proof lens cloth more useful than a real ND filter — wipe between every shot. Bring a microfiber in your jacket pocket.

entries.title: Day 3 (Apr 10) Vík forecast: still windy
entries.message: Per met.no for 63.42,-19.01: Apr 10 Vík = 1-5°C, sustained wind 15.7 m/s, 6 mm rain. Reynisfjara already at orange (viewpoint-only). With 15+ m/s wind on top of orange waves, treat the beach as photo-from-viewpoint only; no walking on sand. Skógafoss still good. Dyrhólaey arch road may be closed in those winds — check road.is morning-of.

entries.title: Qatar Airways status: still corridor-restricted
entries.message: Per gulfnews/aviationoutlook (Apr 1, 2026 schedule): QR running ~120 destinations target by mid-May, up from ~15 at lowest. Doha operating via dedicated airspace corridors. Your booking 7YXVGK (BKK→DOH→BER→KEF) is on the rebuilt schedule so should be fine, but allow extra connection buffer at DOH and check qatarairways.com travel-alerts before BKK departure.

entries.title: Reykjanes volcano: no eruption, magma still building
entries.message: Per vedur.is + visitreykjanes.is: no active eruption. ~21 million m³ magma accumulated beneath Svartsengi since the July 2025 Sundhnúkur eruption ended Aug 5. Most likely scenario in coming weeks = intrusion toward Sundhnúkur crater row possibly leading to eruption. Day 9 Reykjanes/Blue Lagoon plans currently SAFE but monitor — IMO bulletin is the trigger.

entries.title: Heartbeat
entries.message: Loop ran. Open-Meteo still 502; met.no fallback used and confirms forecast. Sources otherwise stable, Kp quiet. Next run in ~2h.

entries.title: Drone — Day 1 likely no-fly
entries.message: Sustained wind 12.9 m/s + gusts ~20 m/s in Reykjavik tomorrow exceed DJI Neo 2's safe envelope (rated to 8 m/s wind resistance). Don't unpack the drone on Day 1; first realistic window is Day 2 Golden Circle if wind eases. Keep batteries warm in inside pocket — cold drains LiPo fast.

entries.title: Day 1 (Apr 8) cross-check via met.no
entries.message: Open-Meteo still 502'd. Fallback met.no shows: 3-5°C, sustained wind 12.9 m/s, 14.9 mm precip. Confirms the wet/windy/cold profile from earlier runs. No significant divergence from prior forecast — Plan B (Perlan/Hallgrímskirkja) still recommended for arrival afternoon.

entries.title: Heartbeat
entries.message: Loop ran. SafeTravel/Reynisfjara stable. Open-Meteo returned 502 this run — will retry next iteration. Kp still quiet (3.33 max). Next run in ~2h.

entries.title: Charge everything before the BKK flight
entries.message: Within hours of QR829 boarding: top up phone, drone batteries (DJI Neo 2 ×3 if you brought spares), power bank, headphones, camera. Doha layover has plug points but they're crowded. Iceland's plug is Type F (Europlug) — pack the adapter in carry-on, not checked.

entries.title: Day 1 storm check vs 25 m/s threshold
entries.message: Latest Open-Meteo for Reykjavik Apr 8: gusts 72 km/h (~20 m/s). Below 25 m/s diversion-risk threshold but right at the upper end of 'gusty.' KEF arrivals should land normally; FI529 BER→KEF historically tolerates much worse. Will keep monitoring.

entries.title: Old Charm Reykjavik: confirm self-check-in code
entries.message: Trip starts tomorrow. Verify Agoda booking es2604070156466592469 has self-check-in instructions/door code in your email. If not visible by 12:00 UTC today, contact host: +354 786 3399 or via Agoda chat. Arrival is late evening Apr 8 — you don't want to be locked out in 72 km/h gusts.

entries.title: Heartbeat
entries.message: Loop ran. Sources stable. Day 1 forecast steady (~72 km/h gusts, 11 mm rain, 4-6°C). Kp slightly up to 3.33 — still below aurora-alert threshold. Next run in ~2h.

entries.title: Sandholt bakery — wet morning fuel near Hallgrímskirkja
entries.message: If Day 1 starts at the church then Plan-B Perlan, walk 4 min downhill to Sandholt (Laugavegur 36) for breakfast — known for sourdough cinnamon snails and proper coffee, opens 07:00. Sheltered indoor seating, perfect for waiting out a rain band. Cards accepted, no cash needed.

entries.title: Heartbeat
entries.message: Loop ran. All sources stable: Day 1 forecast steady (~74 km/h gusts, 9mm rain), Reynisfjara orange, Kp quiet (2.67). Next run in ~2h.

entries.title: Reykjavik wet-day Plan B: Perlan + Hallgrímskirkja tower
entries.message: Given tomorrow's 9mm rain + 74 km/h gusts, swap any outdoor arrival walking for Perlan (planetarium + ice cave exhibit, ~ISK 5990) and Hallgrímskirkja tower (ISK 1400, sheltered elevator view). Both are indoor/semi-indoor, high-reward, and close before 18:00 — check the 20:00 Lava Show slot fits after.

entries.title: Heartbeat
entries.message: Loop ran. Data sources stable since last run. Day 1 still 75 km/h gusts forecast, Kp quiet (max 2.67). Next run in ~2h.

entries.title: Icelandic word of the day: 'rok'
entries.message: 'Rok' = strong wind / gale (rhymes with 'rock'). You'll hear it on every weather update tomorrow. Useful pair: 'það er rok úti' = 'it's blowing a gale outside'. Also 'hvasst' (HVASST) = windy. Locals at the rental counter will respect you using either.

entries.title: Heartbeat
entries.message: Loop ran. SafeTravel/Reynisfjara stable. Day 1 forecast worsened: gusts up to 75 km/h (was 65) — escalated to dangerous-wind warning. Kp quiet. Next run in ~2h.

entries.title: Park into the wind at gas stations
entries.message: With 75 km/h gusts forecast for tomorrow, when stopping at any N1/Olis/etc, point the rental car NOSE into the wind before opening doors. Iceland's #1 rental insurance claim is wind-torn doors and it's never covered. Hold the door with both hands at all stops on Day 1.

entries.title: Day 1 (Apr 8): wind upgraded to dangerous
entries.message: Forecast bumped: gusts now 75 km/h (~21 m/s), sustained 41 km/h (~11 m/s). 21 m/s crosses the dangerous threshold — car doors can be ripped off hinges (Iceland insurance excludes wind damage), and exposed cliff/coast walks (Grótta, Perlan deck) become risky. Plan indoor or sheltered urban Reykjavik for arrival afternoon.

entries.title: Heartbeat
entries.message: Loop ran. No data changes since last run (~2h ago). Day 1 forecast still wet (13.8mm rain, 65 km/h gusts), Kp quiet through Apr 9. Next run in ~2h.

entries.title: Download offline maps before BKK boarding
entries.message: Before QR829 boarding tonight, open Google Maps on your phone and download the 'Iceland' offline region (~700 MB). KEF arrivals WiFi can be slow and rental car pickup needs the route to your hotel — offline tiles save the hassle. Also pin Hertz/SIXT counter and tomorrow's hotel as Saved places.

entries.title: Heartbeat
entries.message: Loop ran. SafeTravel+Reynisfjara unchanged (orange), Day 1 forecast shows wet/gusty Reykjavik arrival, Kp quiet, vedur partial. Next run in ~2h.

entries.title: Pack rain cover now, not at KEF
entries.message: Forecast for tomorrow's Reykjavik arrival is 10mm rain + 66 km/h gusts. Before leaving BKK, move waterproof shell & backpack rain cover to carry-on top layer — you'll want them walking from the FI529 arrivals hall to the rental pickup, not buried in checked luggage.

entries.title: vedur.is check: partial
entries.message: Earthquakes page returned suspicious data (possible fetch artifact) — will retry next run. Warnings page 404 today. No actionable alerts extracted this run.

entries.title: Travel day reminder (Apr 7-8)
entries.message: Check QR829 BKK 20:25 departure on FlightAware before heading to Suvarnabhumi. Gate S111A previously confirmed. Iran/Qatar transit corridor remains the watch item — verify no last-minute rerouting alerts on qatarairways.com.

entries.title: Aurora outlook: quiet
entries.message: NOAA Kp forecast Apr 7-9 nights all <3. No aurora alerts warranted. Will re-check as trip progresses.

entries.title: Day 1 (Apr 8) Reykjavik: wet & gusty arrival
entries.message: Open-Meteo forecast: 4-8°C, 10mm rain, wind max 37 km/h with gusts to 66 km/h (~18 m/s). Expect wet, windy arrival day. Waterproof shell + pack cover essential walking Reykjavik on foot. Exposed sights like Grótta risky in gusts; car doors need firm grip.

entries.title: Reynisfjara: orange (unchanged)
entries.message: Beach accessible from viewpoint only. Sneaker waves not at red. Day 3 (Apr 10) planning should assume viewpoint-only access.

entries.title: SafeTravel pins: no changes
entries.message: 22 active pins unchanged since last fetch.

entries.title: Backup plan for Day 9 (Reykjanes)
entries.message: Given the Svartsengi magma situation, pre-book a refundable Blue Lagoon slot AND sketch a backup Day 9: if Reykjanes closes, you could do Hvalfjörður loop (Glymur trail, cheap, 1h from Reykjavik) + Perlan museum in the afternoon. Keeps the last full day productive even if Blue Lagoon is shut.

entries.title: Heartbeat
entries.message: Loop ran. Pins 22 active (no content change). NOAA Kp quiet (2.67 max 48h). Vedur warnings page 404 again. Volcano page: magma record high under Svartsengi — logged as warning. Flights >24h out, gates stable. Next run ~2h.

entries.title: Svartsengi magma at record high (Day 9 risk)
entries.message: Per vedur.is (update Mar 17, 2026): >23 million m³ magma accumulated beneath Svartsengi — the most since the eruption sequence began. Future Sundhnúkur eruption considered 'likely'. This directly affects Day 9 plans (Apr 16 Reykjanes + Blue Lagoon). Watch for ground-uplift alerts over the next 10 days; have a backup Day 9 itinerary ready (e.g. Reykjavik/Golden Circle revisit).

entries.title: Drone pre-flight check: register DJI Neo 2 in flydrone.is app
entries.message: Nick's operator ID (ISLmded1vbae2qq9-tuu) is valid, but before first flight in Iceland confirm the DJI Neo 2 is added to your aircraft list in the flydrone.is app and you have the A1 sub-250g category selected. Also pre-download the no-fly-zone map offline — mobile data in rural Iceland can be spotty around Snæfellsnes.

entries.title: vedur.is check: all quiet
entries.message: Warnings page returned 404 (likely temporary). Aurora outlook tonight poor (activity 2/9 + extensive cloud cover). Largest quakes M3.7-3.9 at Flatey/Grímsey in N. Iceland — far from Reykjanes, no volcanic implication.

entries.title: Loop heartbeat: all tasks checked, nothing notable
entries.message: Pins: no content change (22 active). Weather: skipped (pre-trip, Apr 6). Aurora: quiet, max Kp 2.67 next 48h. Flights: skipped (not a travel day). One-shots: none.

entries.title: Loop run: pre-trip checks OK
entries.message: SafeTravel pins refreshed (no content changes, 22 active). Aurora forecast quiet (max Kp 2.67 next 48h, well below visible threshold). Pre-trip window — weather/flight tasks skipped. T-1 day until BKK→DOH→BER→KEF travel (Apr 7-8).

entries.title: Message board initialized
entries.message: Loop will post status updates here
